scenario: Meridian Bank operates Camunda 8.8 with 22000 customers and 18000 payments per day. After a faulty exclusive gateway pushed 1800 payment instances down a misconfigured branch, the operations lead has a 2 hours window to launch a batch move from the wrong service task to the corrected one, using Operate only and without scripting any zbctl calls.
question: Which sequence of steps correctly starts batch modification mode for those 1800 affected instances in Operate?

options.text: Open the Operations panel, choose a target flow node, click Apply Modification, then begin selecting the affected source instances inside the confirmation modal that opens.
Select the source flow node, choose the affected instances in the list view, click Move when the toolbar appears, then click Continue on the information modal to enter batch mode.
Click Move from the process diagram panel before selecting any instances so that every active instance on the source flow node is auto-batched into a single grouped move.
Use the Operations panel filter, click Discard to clear stale entries, then click Move to load a fresh batch selection containing every active instance on that flow node.

a.text: Incorrect. The docs entry to batch mode does not start in the Operations panel; the operator must first select a source flow node and individual instances, click Move, then Continue on the information modal.

b.text: Correct. The docs describe exactly this sequence: select the flow node containing the instances to move, select the instances in the list view so the Move action appears in the toolbar, click Move, then click Continue on the information modal to enter batch modification mode.

c.text: Incorrect. Clicking Move before selecting instances does not auto-batch every active instance; the Move action becomes available only after instances are selected and the toolbar appears.

d.text: Incorrect. Discard is used inside batch mode to drop currently selected instances, not as part of the entry sequence, and the docs do not describe loading a batch from the Operations panel filter.

explanation: To enter batch modification mode the operator selects the source flow node, picks the relevant process instances in the list view so the Move action appears in the toolbar, clicks Move, then clicks Continue on the information modal that confirms the switch to batch move mode.
